title: Weight area chart with time filter toggle
description: Build the area chart that plots the user's weight over time using Recharts, with 4 time filter toggle buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Chart renders as a smooth area chart with weight on Y-axis and date on X-axis
4 filter buttons: 'Last 7 Days', 'Last 30 Days', 'Last 3 Months', 'All Time'
Active filter button is visually highlighted
Selecting a filter re-fetches data and updates the chart
Chart shows an empty state message when no entries exist for the selected range
Chart re-fetches when refreshKey changes (new entry added)

steps.details: Create src/web/src/components/WeightChart.tsx. Props: refreshKey (number). State: activeFilter ('7d' | '30d' | '3m' | 'all'), entries (WeightEntry[]), isLoading (boolean). On mount and when filter/refreshKey changes, call getWeightEntries({ time_filter: activeFilter, page: 1, page_size: 1000 }) — use a large page size to get all entries for the chart.

Render 4 buttons using shadcn/ui Button: variant='outline' for inactive, variant='default' for active. Labels: 'Last 7 Days', 'Last 30 Days', 'Last 3 Months', 'All Time'. Clicking a button sets activeFilter state.

Use ResponsiveContainer width='100%' height={300}. Inside: AreaChart with data mapped from entries to { date: entry.recorded_at, weight: entry.weight_value }. Add CartesianGrid, XAxis (dataKey='date', formatted as DD.MM), YAxis (domain set to [min-5, max+5] for visual padding), Tooltip, Area (type='monotone', dataKey='weight', stroke primary color, fill with opacity). Show a centered 'No data for this period' message when entries is empty.

With weight entries logged, take a screenshot of the chart. Click each filter button and verify the chart updates. Verify the active filter button is highlighted. Test with no entries in the selected range and verify the empty state message.

technicalNotes: Recharts requires numeric data for Area — ensure weight values are numbers not strings
Sort entries by recorded_at ascending for the chart (oldest to newest left to right)
Use a page_size of 1000 when fetching for the chart — this is sufficient for a personal tracker
Recharts ResponsiveContainer needs a parent with explicit height or width
